items.title: Corporate America’s hefty profit streak continues amid worries over job market
items.description: Earnings Watch: Even as fretting over the economy remains a pastime on Wall Street, third-quarter corporate profit growth has held up, helped by big banks and big tech.

items.title: Palantir, AMD, Pfizer, Robinhood, McDonald’s, and Many More Stocks to Watch This Week
items.description: Vertex, Corteva, and many more will also report earnings. On the economic front, The Supreme Court will hear arguments on the legality of Trump’s tariffs, and ADP will release October employment data.

items.title: How Domino's Pizza Earned a Place in Berkshire Hathaway's Portfolio
items.author: William Dahl
items.description: Berkshire Hathaway has been steadily acquiring Domino's Pizza shares despite challenging macroeconomic conditions, attracted by the company's strong dividend growth, consistent earnings, and share buyback strategy.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock performance is flat, with 20% decline since summer 2024, but strong earnings growth (21.5%) and consistent dividend increases suggest underlying resilience
Actively acquiring Domino's shares, indicating confidence in the company's long-term potential and dividend growth strategy
